mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals a labor market inflection that historically precedes a 20-day equity drawdown as earnings revisions turn negative.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in real GDP growth and a federal funds rate above 4.0% creates a liquidity-demand mismatch that compresses equity multiples, driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 55% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a sustained rise in industrial production above 103.0 index points drives positive equity momentum over the next week as capacity utilization translates to earnings upgrades.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) when INDPRO exceeds 103.0 during the specified recovery window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers immediate repricing of inflation expectations, forcing equity markets to discount future cash flows at higher rates and generating negative 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for CPIAUCSL monthly changes remains above 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, contradicting the negative return prediction.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls growth exceeds 200,000 while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises above 4.2%, it indicates a labor market expansion driven by new entrants rather than wage pressure, which historically supports positive 60-day equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal produces a directional accuracy below 50% over a 10-year walk-forward test, indicating no predictive edge.

mechanism_narrative: A consistent upward trajectory in retail sales and food services over consecutive months reflects robust consumer spending, which directly lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising RSAFS periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle where the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5%, a decline in the unemployment rate below 4.0% signals an overheating labor market that historically precedes a 60-day equity correction as the Fed accelerates policy restraint.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances where UNRATE falls below 4.0% and FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%, invalidating the negative return prediction.
